ref: hippo-product-doc.pdf p.1 — The Voice Stand-In for Modern Teams
text:
## The Voice Stand-In for Modern Teams
Hippo is a voice agent that joins meetings when you can't — speaking in your cloned voice and answering questions about your work, grounded in your Slack, Linear, and documents.
# Image Description

The image appears to be a testimonial-style graphic with a quote at the top and a figure caption area below.

## Top Quote Section

There is a light gray background with a vertical blue bar along the far left edge.

The visible quote text reads:

> "Hippo is what I always wished Slack-status updates could be. My team didn't even realize I was out."  
> — Maya Chen, Engineering Lead, Northwind Labs

The quote is styled in italic text.

## Lower Figure/Caption Section

Below the quote section is a horizontal area separated by whitespace.

On the left side, there is what appears to be a broken or unloaded image placeholder inside a dashed rectangular border. Next to the small image icon, the visible alt-style text reads:

**Hero: a hippo wearing AirPods sitting in a Zoom call window**

To the right of that, italicized caption text reads:

**Figure 1. Hippo joining a Tuesday standup**

Below that, continuing on the next line, the remaining caption text reads:

**on behalf of an absent engineer.**

## Why Hippo Exists
Async updates don't scale. Written standups go unread. Recordings are skipped. The information your teammates actually need lives in Linear tickets, Slack threads, and design docs — but no one has time to dig.
Hippo closes the gap. When you're OOO, on a flight, or simply heads-down, your Hippo agent shows up in the meeting and answers questions the way you would, with the data you'd cite, in the voice your teammates know.

| v0.4 | Mar 22, 2026 | Pod | Multi-source semantic memory (Moss) |

| Version | Release Date | Codename | Headline |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| v1.0 | Jun 07, 2026 | River | Voice cloning GA — production ready |
| Bar chart of weekly active meetings handled, climbing from 12 (Feb) to 1,847 (Jun) Figure 2. Weekly meetings handled by Hippo across pilot customers. | | | |

ref: hippo-product-doc.pdf p.2 — What's New in v1.0 "River"
text:
## What's New in v1.0 "River"
The headline of v1.0 is voice cloning at production quality. After eight weeks of beta with Minimax's neutral TTS, we've shipped Qwen-powered voice cloning that's indistinguishable from the speaker in 87% of blind A/B tests.

## Shipped Features
- Voice Cloning (GA). 90-second enrollment. One-time consent flow. Cloned voice is encrypted at rest and tied to the speaker's account.
- Live Q&A. Hippo answers unscripted follow-up questions in real time — retrieval latency dropped from 1.8s (v0.5) to 340ms median.
- Multi-source grounding. Slack, Linear, Notion, and uploaded PDFs all indexed into a single Moss namespace per user.
- Post-meeting briefs. Hippo writes a summary to a Slack channel of your choice within 30 seconds of meeting end, including action items addressed to you.
- Calendar integration. Hippo auto-detects when you're OOO and joins meetings on your behalf without manual setup.
- Speaker permissioning. Each user controls which channels Hippo can speak from and which colleagues it will speak to.

## Performance Benchmarks
| Metric | v0.5 (Beta) | v1.0 (GA) | Δ |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Median retrieval latency | 1,820 ms | 340 ms | -81% |
| Voice clone MOS (1–5 scale) | n/a | 4.3 | — |
| Answer accuracy (eval set N=400) | 71% | 89% | +18 pts |
| Meeting drop rate | 4.2% | 0.3% | -93% |
| Architecture diagram: LiveKit room → Hippo agent → Moss retrieval → Qwen voice synthesis Figure 3.   Hippo v1.0 system architecture. | | | |

ref: hippo-product-doc.pdf p.3 — Known Issues at GA
text:
## Known Issues at GA
We're shipping v1.0 with full transparency. The following are tracked and on the roadmap:
| Issue | Severity | Workaround |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Voice clone drifts on speakers with strong accents | Low | Re-enroll with a 3-minute sample |
| Linear comments older than 90 days not indexed | Low | Manual re-sync via dashboard |
| Hippo will not interrupt the active speaker | By design | Raise hand via slash command in chat |
| Rare crosstalk artifacts in 4+ participant calls | Medium | Patch shipping in v1.0.1 (Jun 14) |

## Pricing
| Plan | Price | Best for |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Solo | $20 / user / mo | Individual ICs, freelancers |
| Team | $40 / user / mo | Engineering teams up to 50 |
| Enterprise | Custom | SSO, audit logs, on-prem voice |

ref: hippo-product-doc.pdf p.3 — What Customers Are Saying
text:
## What Customers Are Saying
"Our standups used to take 25 minutes for 8 people. With Hippo handling the three who are always rolling between calls, we're down to 12 minutes and getting better updates." — Devon Park, VP Engineering, Lattice Bio

"I was skeptical of the voice clone until I heard my CTO's Hippo answer a roadmap question with a quote I literally said in 1:1 last week. He's now our most reliable meeting attendee." — Anonymous, Series B fintech

Three customer logos arranged in a row Figure 4. Pilot customers.

## What's Next
The team is heads-down on v1.1 (codename **Delta**), targeting late July 2026. Priorities:
- 1. Real-time translation — Hippo speaks in your voice, in another language.
- 2. Multi-meeting memory — Hippo references prior meetings, not just docs.
- 3. Mobile app — see Hippo's meeting transcripts and corrections on the go.
But for today: Hippo v1.0 is feature-complete, performance-validated, and ready for the market. Voice cloning is live, retrieval is fast, and the post-meeting brief makes the absent teammate's contribution visible to the whole team.
We're done. Time to ship.
Hippo is built on LiveKit, Moss, Qwen, Minimax, Unsiloed, and TrueFoundry. Hippo, Inc. — San Francisco, CA — hippo.ai
